data.description:
## Why

`verify.completion_floor_active` was assessed as fully implemented (no stubs; wired live
into `review_plan` at `llm/plan_review/__init__.py:536`) AND already calibrated: its
gold-set story 77cf is CLOSED, and `docs/calibration/completion_floor.md` records 30/30
floor decisions correct (κ=1.0) with zero must-never-suppress anchors dropped — stated
verbatim as "the evidence the operator needs before flipping the flag on". It has only
never been switched on. Enable it in THIS project to dogfood it on live reviews.

## What

- Set `verify.completion_floor_active = true` in this project's `rebar.toml` (schema
  default in `_config_schema.py:303` STAYS False for clients). Keep the companion tunables
  at their defaults (`completion_priority_floor = 0.4`, `completion_preserve_criteria =
  T5c,T10`) unless dogfooding evidence says otherwise.
- Identify + document the monitoring surface: every suppression is persisted to the
  plan-review SIDECAR `dropped[]` tagged `drop_reason="completion"` with full completion
  answers + `coverage.completion_floored_finding_ids` (`llm/plan_review/__init__.py:263,
  272-275`; `sidecar.py:342,358`). Document how to tail/audit it so a wrongly-suppressed
  finding is recoverable.
- OPTIONAL (judgment call, capture as a follow-up if declined): add a per-drop
  `logger.info` line at the drop site so live suppressions are visible without opening the
  sidecar — the successful-drop path currently logs nothing (only failures warn).

## Scope

Config activation + monitoring only — NO logic change to the floor. Risk is bounded:
advisory-only, container-only, 5-condition conjunction, fail-safe-to-keep everywhere. A
wrong suppression cannot change a PASS/BLOCK verdict and is always recoverable from the
sidecar. Rollback = set the key back to false.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `verify.completion_floor_active = true` present in this project's `rebar.toml`; schema default remains False.
- [ ] A short operator note (in `docs/calibration/completion_floor.md` or the plan-review-gate doc) explains how to audit completion-floor suppressions via the sidecar `dropped[]` / `drop_reason="completion"`.
- [ ] A re-fired epic/story plan-review demonstrably runs the floor (a drop appears in the sidecar, or a documented case shows none qualified) without changing any PASS/BLOCK verdict.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass (the floor's existing unit/e2e tests stay green).
data.title: Enable verify.completion_floor_active in this project (dogfood) + monitoring
